description: Full derivation of wa_meaning_stem rows and wa_meaning_parsed updates from STEP meaning blocks. Also records causative_form_present corrections and wa_meaning_parsed rows to be created for terms that lack them.

wa_meaning_stem_insert.top_sense_text: to detest, make abominable, count filthy, make detestable
to loathe, abhor, regard as an abomination
to have indignation, be indignant, be angrily indignant, be defiant
to stink, emit a stinking odour
to be made to writhe, be made to bear
to be angry with, be incensed
to become aroused, inflame oneself with
to be wroth, to be full of wrath, to be furious
to provoke to wrath or anger
to put oneself in a rage, anger oneself
to cause to fall, fell, throw down
: to dance, to twist/writhe, to whirl
: to dance, to writhe in travail/bear, to wait anxiously
: to be made to writhe/bear, to be brought forth
: whirling, writhing/suffering torture, to wait longingly
to be at ease, be at peace, rest, rest securely, be quiet
to love, be attached to, long for
to ask for oneself, ask leave of absence
to desire, long for, lust after (of bodily appetites)
: to lift up; to bear, carry, support, sustain, endure; to take, take away, carry off, forgive
: to be lifted up, be exalted; to lift oneself up, rise up; to be borne, carried; to be taken away
: to lift up, exalt, support, aid, assist; to desire, long (fig.); to carry, bear continuously; to take away
to lift oneself up, exalt oneself
: to cause one to bear (iniquity); to cause to bring, have brought
: to long for (deeply); being longed for (participle)
: to gasp, pant after, snuff up; to gasp or pant (with desire), pant after, be eager for; to thirst for one's blood (from actions of animals) (fig)

: human love for another, includes family, and sexual; human appetite for objects such as food, drink, sleep, wisdom; human love for or to God; act of being a friend; lover (participle); friend (parti
: to seek to find; to seek to secure; to seek the face; to desire, demand; to require, exact; to ask, request
to spare, pity, have compassion on
to provoke to jealous anger, cause jealousy
to watch stealthily or with envious hostility, watch with enmity, watch with envy
to be anxious, to dread; used of anxious fear about impending calamity or uncertain outcome.
to be astonished, stare in amazement.
to dread, stand in awe of; STEP subdivided sense H1481C - the fear sense of the root gur. H1481A is the primary sense (to sojourn). The two senses share the same spelling.
to be in dread, to be in awe; (Piel) to be in great dread; (Hiphil) to cause to dread. The verb form of pa.chad (H6343). Covers both instinctive terror and reverential awe.
to cause to be afraid, make afraid. Used exclusively in the Aramaic sections of Daniel. Related to Hebrew za.chal.
to fear, dread. The verb form corresponding to the adjective fearing (ya.gor H3016). Used of personal dread and fearful apprehension.
to fear; meaning uncertain; hapax legomenon. Occurs only in Isa 44:8, paired with ya.re in a context of reassurance. The precise semantic distinction from ya.re is unclear.

to fear, be afraid; to stand in awe of, be awed; (Niphal) to be fearful, dreadful, be feared; to cause astonishment and awe; to inspire reverence or godly fear; (Piel) to make afraid, terrify. STEP su
to hasten; (Niphal) to be hurried, be anxious; (Piel) to hasten, make haste, prepare quickly. The fear-relevant sense is the Niphal: to be anxious/hurried. STEP H4116A is a subdivided sense.

to storm, be stormy; (Niphal) to be enraged; (Piel) to storm away; (Pual) to be driven by storm. Included in the fear word list via the association of storm/rage with dread. Primary meaning is meteoro

to waste away; (Niphal) to melt, vanish, drop off, faint, grow fearful; (Hiphil) to cause to melt. The fear sense arises from the Niphal: to grow fearful, to faint with fear. Used frequently of hearts
to tremble of mountains or people, to be anxiously careful, to go trembling; (Hiphil) to cause to tremble, to drive in terror, rout. A strongly somatic term - the physical act of trembling induced by
to cause to tremble, terrify; (Niphal) to be awesome, terrible; (Hiphil) to regard with awe, terrify. Covers both the experience of terror and the causing of it.
to be or become weak, feel weak
to be made weak, become weak
to be in pain, be pained, be grieved
to do wrong, offend, trespass, commit an offense, do injury
to be lifted up, be exalted
to lift up, exalt, support, aid, assist
to lift oneself up, exalt oneself
to miss oneself, lose oneself, wander from the way
to wait or look eagerly for
to seek refuge, flee for protection
to cause to trust, make secure
to lay or bring low, humiliate
to do well, do good, act right, act rightly
to be shown compassion, be compassionate
: 1a1) to be silent; 1a2) to be still, die; 1a3) to be struck dumb. 1b) (Niphal) to be silenced, be made silent, destroyed. 1c) (Poal) to make quiet. 1d) (Hiphil) to make silent (cause to die).
to wait for. 1b) (Piel): 1b1) to wait, tarry; 1b2) to wait (in ambush); 1b3) to wait for, long for.
to wait. 1b) (Piel): 1b1) to wait, await, tarry; 1b2) to wait for, hope for. 1c) (Hiphil) to wait, tarry, wait for, hope for. Also means: ya.chal — hope (H3176H).
to be weary, be impatient. 1b) (Niphal) to be tired of something, weary oneself. 1c) (Hiphil) to weary, make weary, exhaust.
to be long. 1b) (Hiphil): 1b1) to prolong (days); 1b2) to make long (tent cords); 1b3) to grow long, continue long. Aramaic equivalent: a.rakh (H0749) — be proper.
to act insolently, storm against, beset, importune
to disturb, alarm, awe, confuse, make bold or proud
to be sorry, be moved to pity, have compassion
to be sorry, be moved to pity, have compassion
to cause to return, bring back
to be returned, be restored, be brought back
to cause to return, bring back
to be returned, be restored, be brought back
to cause to return, bring back
to be returned, be restored, be brought back
to cause to return, bring back
to be returned, be restored, be brought back
to be ashamed before one another
to display shame, demonstrate shame, cause embarrassment
to put to shame, insult, humiliate, cause shame to
to be put to shame, be reproached
: to profane oneself, defile oneself, pollute oneself (ritually or sexually); to be polluted, be defiled
: to profane, make common, defile, pollute; to violate the honour of, dishonour; to violate (a covenant); to treat as common
: to be profaned (of name of God)
: to let be profaned; to begin
poem, song or poem of contemplation. A Maskil of David.
to look at or upon, have insight
to be or become wise, act wisely
to make wise, teach wisdom, instruct
to show oneself wise, deceive, show one's wisdom
to take heart, become enheartened, become intelligent
to ravish the heart, encourage, make heart beat faster
to breathe, blow; 1b) (Pual) to be blown; 1c) (Hiphil) to cause to breathe out.
— 1a1) to go or come out or forth, depart; 1a2) to go forth to a place; 1a3) to go forward, proceed; 1a4) to come or go forth with purpose or result; 1a5) to come out of; 1b) (Hiphil) — 1b1) to cause
to leap, jump, spring, leap for joy
to cause to rejoice, gladden, make glad
to cause to rejoice, gladden, make glad
to shout, cry aloud, cry out
to give a ringing cry (in joy, exaltation, distress)
to give a ringing cry (in joy, exultation, praise)
to cause to ring or sing out (for joy)
to do an injury or hurt
to be disturbed, be dismayed, be terrified, be hurried, be anxious
to be in pain, be hurt, be grieved
to confine, shut in, besiege, bind
to feel a loathing, dread, abhor
to be hard, be difficult, be severe
to meditate, muse, complain, talk, sing, speak
: to be silent; to be deaf
: to be silent, keep quiet; to make silent; to be deaf, show deafness
: to be silent, exhibit silence; to show inactivity, be inactive; to make silent, make quiet, cause to be still
: to be complete; to be finished, be ended; to be at peace
: to complete, finish; to make safe; to make whole or good, restore; to reward, recompense, requite
: to make complete; to make peace with; to cause to be at peace
: to be complete; to be finished, be ended; to be at peace
: to complete, finish; to make safe; to make whole or good, restore; to reward, recompense, requite
: to make complete; to make peace with; to cause to be at peace
: to cut in, engrave; to plow; to devise
: to be quiet, be undisturbed; to be at peace, be quiet
: to cause quietness, cause to be at peace; to quiet, give rest
: to rest, settle down and remain; to repose, have rest, be quiet
: to cause to rest, give rest to; to set down, deposit, let lie
: to be caused to rest, be deposited
: to cause to rest (of sword, of the sea); to wink (with prep)
: to give rest to; to rest, repose
to equip for war, to arm; (Niphal) to be equipped, to go equipped for battle
to be strong, to grow strong
to insert, thrust, thrust in, thrust upon
to be fearful, be dreadful, be feared
to sojourn, dwell for a time
to be shattered, be dismayed, be scared
to be made heavy, be honoured, enjoy honour, be made abundant
to make heavy, make dull, make insensible
to be made honourable, be honoured
to make oneself heavy, make oneself dense, make oneself numerous
to call, cry, utter a loud sound
to be called, be named, be called out, be chosen
to be sorry, be moved to pity, have compassion
to be set apart, be consecrated
to show oneself sacred or majestic
to set apart as sacred, consecrate, dedicate
to keep oneself apart or separate
to advise, counsel, give counsel, consult
to consult together, exchange counsel, deliberate, counsel together
to be or become king or queen, reign
to make one king or queen, cause to reign
to be made king or queen
to fix or seat themselves close together, sit in conclave
to occur, come to pass, be done, be brought about
to be of dainty habit, be pampered
to be smeared over, be blinded
to besmear (of eyes), smear over eyes
to smell, scent, perceive odour, accept
to be pleased with, be favourable to
to be accepted, be pleased with
to make oneself acceptable or pleasing
to be pleasant, be beautiful, be sweet, be delightful, be lovely
to support, confirm, be faithful, uphold, nourish
to be established, be faithful, be carried, make firm
to stand firm, to trust, to be certain, to believe in
to sojourn, dwell for a time
to do an injury or hurt
to be caused to see, be shown
to look at each other, face
to act or deal treacherously, faithlessly, deceitfully, offend

to give, bestow, grant, permit, ascribe, employ, devote, consecrate, dedicate, pay wages, sell, exchange, lend, commit, entrust, give over, deliver up, yield produce, occasion, produce, requite to, re
to be given, be bestowed, be provided, be entrusted to, be granted to, be permitted, be issued, be published, be uttered, be assigned
to be given, be bestowed, be given up, be delivered up
to make foolish, turn into foolishness
to do foolishly, play the fool
sigh, groan (in pain or grief), gasp
to be set apart, be consecrated
to show oneself sacred or majestic
to set apart as sacred, consecrate, dedicate
to keep oneself apart or separate
to be bent, be bowed down, be twisted, be perverted
to look at or upon, have insight
to be discerning, intelligent, discreet, have understanding
to show oneself discerning or attentive, consider diligently
to act as law-giver or judge or governor (of God, man)
to enter into controversy, plead, have controversy together
to stretch out, extend, stretch, offer
to have a just cause, be in the right
to be put or made right, be justified
justify, make to appear righteous, make someone righteous
to do or bring justice (in administering law)
to be made known, be or become known, be revealed
to make oneself known, reveal oneself
roving, ranging, longing, rush out upon (participle)
to rush to and fro, rush back and forth
to make gracious, make favourable, be gracious
to direct favour to, have mercy on
to be shown favour, be shown consideration
symbolic name given by the prophet Hosea to his daughter
to be sorry, be moved to pity, have compassion
to cast, cast down, throw down
to come to an end, be no more
to laugh (usually in contempt or derision)
to be fearful, be dreadful, be feared
to resort to, frequent (a place), (tread a place)
to allow oneself to be enquired of, consulted (only of God)
to deal in integrity, act uprightly
to be a tale-bearer, slander, go about

to give, bestow, grant, permit, ascribe, employ, devote, consecrate, dedicate, pay wages, sell, exchange, lend, commit, entrust, give over, deliver up, yield produce, occasion, produce, requite to, re

to be given, be bestowed, be given up, be delivered up
to practice witchcraft or sorcery, use witchcraft
to sojourn, dwell for a time
to go or come out or forth, depart
to cause to go or come out, bring out, lead out
to be brought out or forth
to say, to answer, to say in one's heart, to think, to command, to promise, to intend
to be told, to be said, to be called
to be accounted, be thought, be esteemed
to think upon, consider, be mindful of
to look at or upon, have insight
to be brought to remembrance, be remembered, be thought of, be brought to mind
to be smooth, be shiny, gleam

to take heart, become enheartened, become intelligent
to ravish the heart, encourage, make heart beat faster
to be a harlot, act as a harlot, commit fornication
to condemn as guilty (in civil relations)
to be empty, be clean, be pure
to be cleaned out, be purged out
break, break in or down, rend violently, wreck, crush, quench
to be broken, be maimed, be crippled, be wrecked
to cause to break out, bring to the birth
to go, walk, come, depart, proceed, move, go away
to lead, bring, lead away, carry, cause to walk

to do extraordinary or hard or difficult thing
to show oneself wonderful or marvellous

to be led or enticed to serve

to lead, bring, lead away, carry, cause to walk
to be taken up, be brought up, be taken away

to be slight, be abated (of water)

to regard or treat as foolish
to be dishonoured, be despised, be lightly esteemed
to treat with contempt, dishonour, treat shamefully
